title: sideritis borgiae subsp. relegata
intro: Sideritis borgiae subsp. relegata is a subspecies of the Sideritis genus, commonly known as mountain tea or ironwort, a type of flowering plant native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Sideritis borgiae subsp. relegata include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Sideritis borgiae subsp. relegata can be prepared as a herbal tea by infusing the dried leaves and flowers in hot water, or by using it in tincture or capsule form to extract its medicinal properties.
